verdict_explanation: The methodology (SCED / N-of-1 trial design) is 40-60 year old prior art from psychology and medicine. The specific application to AI agent memory deployment, the necessary adaptations (irreversible carryover, session-boundary phase definition, LLM-specific DVs), and the construction of a measurement instrument are novel. No paper found has bridged SCED/N-of-1 into the AI agent memory domain.

sources.title: Single-Case Research Designs: Methods for Clinical and Applied Settings (2nd ed.)
sources.author: Kazdin, A.E.
sources.analogy: Their 'single subject as own control across repeated phases with visual analysis of level/trend/variability' = our proposed repeated-measurement deployment design for one user with memory-on/off phase logic
sources.notes: Canonical SCED reference. Explicitly covers clinical settings, technology-mediated interventions, and maintenance of behavior — all directly applicable.

sources.title: Personalized (N-of-1) Trials: A Primer
sources.author: Kravitz et al.
sources.analogy: Their 'repeated crossover within one patient using time-series analysis, within-subject correlation parameter rho, and meta-analytic aggregation across trials' = our statistical framework for longitudinal n=1 agent memory evaluation
sources.notes: N-of-1 without washout (irreversible memory) requires adaptation — this is the novel contribution we would make.

sources.title: N-of-1 trials: The epitome of personalized medicine?
sources.author: Multiple authors
sources.analogy: Their 'individual response heterogeneity requires within-person evidence rather than population averages' = our core argument that group AI benchmarks miss individual memory utility
sources.notes: Philosophical alignment near-perfect. The 'idiosyncratic response' framing maps precisely to why aggregate benchmarks fail.

sources.title: N-of-1 Design and Its Applications to Personalized Treatment Studies
sources.author: Multiple authors
sources.analogy: Their 'composite endpoint combining crossover phase and extension phase data' = our potential design combining memory-toggle experiment with open-ended longitudinal deployment
sources.notes: Carryover management via washout is inapplicable to cumulative memory — this is the key adaptation challenge.

sources.title: Developer Productivity With and Without GitHub Copilot: A Longitudinal Mixed-Methods Case Study
sources.author: Multiple authors
sources.analogy: Their '2-year before/after longitudinal study of AI tool adoption at NAV IT' = closest SE precedent for our design, but uses group-level inference (n=25), not n=1
sources.notes: Key finding (perceived ≠ measured productivity) motivates n=1 design rather than falsifying it. Not n=1 methodology.

sources.title: Offline Recommender System Evaluation: Challenges and New Directions
sources.author: Jannach et al.
sources.analogy: Their 'offline metrics fail to predict individual-user online outcomes; longitudinal evaluation with humans in the loop needed' = our argument for real-deployment n=1 measurement over static benchmarks
sources.notes: Identifies identical methodological problem but does not propose n=1 solution. Confirms the gap.

sources.title: Personal Tracking as Lived Informatics
sources.author: Rooksby et al.
sources.analogy: Their 'individual longitudinal self-tracking with reflection and action cycles' = our framework for a user observing, reflecting on, and adjusting their use of a memory-augmented agent
sources.notes: HCI precedent for individual longitudinal study design. Not formal SCED; no statistical inference framework.

sources.title: Replicability Measures for Longitudinal Information Retrieval Evaluation
sources.author: Multiple authors
sources.analogy: Their 'temporal evolution of queries and relevance requires longitudinal evaluation beyond static Cranfield paradigm' = our 'temporal evolution of user-memory interaction requires deployment evaluation beyond static AI benchmarks'
sources.notes: IR field's version of the benchmark-vs-deployment tension. Corpus-level, not user-level.

sources.title: Beyond Static Evaluation: Rethinking the Assessment of Personalized Agent Adaptability in Information Retrieval
sources.author: Multiple authors
sources.analogy: Their 'temporally evolving simulated personas with longitudinal feedback loops' = closest AI-side analog to our real-deployment n=1 design
sources.notes: Uses simulated, not real users. Does not use SCED/N-of-1 framing. If extended to real deployment it would be strong prior art.

sources.title: Single-Case Experimental Designs: A Systematic Review of Published Research and Current Standards
sources.author: Kratochwill et al.
sources.analogy: Their 'WWC standards for SCED: minimum phase lengths, inter-rater reliability, visual analysis criteria' = reporting standards we would adapt for AI memory deployment studies
sources.notes: Provides the reporting and quality standards we would adopt and adapt. Not falsifying for application novelty.
